name: HOLLYWOOD
clue: Cowboy star who has 5 stars on Hollywood's Walk of Fame—1 each for film, TV, recording, theater & radio

name: HISTORY & THE MOVIES
clue: This 1935 Best Picture Oscar winner tells of a 1789 event near the isolated Pacific volcano of Tofua

name: OPERA

name: SPORTING EVENTS

name: COUNTRIES OF THE WORLD

clue: Since 1850, 1 of the 2 Republicans to appear on the ticket as president or vice president 3 elections in a row

clue: Translator Edward Fitzgerald wrote that her 1861 "death is rather a relief to me... no more Aurora Leighs, thank God\"

name: U.S. PRESIDENTS

name: WORLD CITIES
clue: In 1634 a Spanish royal decree recognized it as the "Key to the New World and the Bulwark of the West Indies\"

name: THE CALENDAR

name: WORD ORIGINS

name: PRESIDENTIAL RELATIVES

clue: King Agamemnon was killed on his return to Greece after ignoring the warnings of this woman, his slave

clue: This state with several hundred ghost towns was admitted to the Union on Halloween in 1864

clue: On Sept. 8, 1565 the first Catholic parish in what is now the U.S. was founded at this settlement

name: ARCHITECTURE
clue: Begun in the 1170s on former marshland, it has been called a "perfect imperfection" & a "legendary mistake\"

name: CAPITAL CITY WORDPLAY
clue: Ending in the same 2 letters, these 2 are capitals of a nation that covers a continent & of a nation reaching onto 2 continents

clue: Everything changed in 1924 after he observed, "I have found a Cepheid variable in the Andromeda Nebula\"

clue: In 1998, the highest-ranking person in the line of presidential succession who could not legally be president

name: TELEVISION
clue: It's the longest-running prime time sports show in the history of network television

name: WORD ORIGINS
clue: Where you'd naturally find armalcolite, named for the 3 men who 1st handled it

name: COUNTRIES OF THE WORLD
clue: Of the South American countries, the one that comes last alphabetically

clue: This title character of the top song from 1996 can't stand her boyfriend Vitorino & spurns him to be with his 2 friends

name: LITERARY QUOTES
clue: This title is the only quote by playwright John Osborne in Bartlett's

clue: This man is the only U.S. president since Hoover not named Time magazine's Man or Person of the Year

name: COUNTRIES OF THE WORLD

name: OSCAR NOMINEES
clue: The last 2 sisters nominated against each other for the Best Actress Oscar; it was for 1966

name: LITERATURE
